summaryText: Guest speaker Pastor Tauren Wells opened with a joyful greeting from his home church in Austin, Texas, and expressed deep gratitude for the Impact Church family. He centered his message on Matthew 5:1-12, the Beatitudes, framing it as Jesus's most important message on joy in human history. Pastor Wells challenged the common religious reflex that God is indifferent to our happiness, arguing instead that God's very nature is infinite joy and that He is uniquely invested in our flourishing. He explained that the word 'blessed' used by Jesus translates to 'happy,' signifying a prosperous, glad, and flourishing life rooted in Heaven's principles. The sermon contrasted the Old Testament's ending word, 'curse,' with Jesus's opening word, 'blessed,' repositioning God's original intention toward humanity. Wells emphasized that true, spiritual happiness is found only in a right relationship with Jesus and cannot be fulfilled by vocational, social, or physical happiness alone. He illustrated that joy is not reserved for spectacular 'mountaintop' moments but can be found in ordinary, desolate, or isolated places ('Aramis'), as Jesus demonstrated by teaching on a simple hillside. The message concluded with a powerful call to 'reintroduce yourself to joy' by connecting it to Christ, the true source, and an invitation for salvation for those meeting Jesus for the first time.
mainPoints: Your happiness matters to God; He is a God of infinite joy who desires your flourishing, as demonstrated by Jesus's first word in the Beatitudes being 'blessed' (happy).
True, lasting joy (spiritual happiness) is found only in a relationship with Jesus Christ; other forms of happiness cannot fill that void.
Joy is not dependent on spectacular circumstances but can be found and forged in ordinary, desolate, or isolated places, as we choose to rename our pain with God's strength and perspective.

versesMentioned.text: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he went up on a mountainside and sat down. His disciples came to him, and he began to teach them. He said: 'Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven. Blessed are those who mourn, for they will be comforted. Blessed are the meek, for they will inherit the earth. Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they will be filled. Blessed are the merciful, for they will be shown mercy. Blessed are the pure in heart, for they will see God. Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called children of God. Blessed are those who are persecuted because of righteousness, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.'

God blessed them and said to them, 'Be fruitful and increase in number; fill the earth and subdue it. Rule over the fish in the sea and the birds in the sky and over every living creature that moves on the ground.'
As she breathed her last—for she was dying—she named her son Ben-Oni. But his father named him Benjamin.

Monday.scripture: Matthew 5:3 (NIV) - 'Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.'
Monday.action: Begin your day with a prayer acknowledging your need for God's strength and wisdom.
Monday.prayer: Lord, I come to you poor in spirit. I need you today. Help me to rely on your Kingdom's resources, not my own.

Tuesday.scripture: Matthew 5:4 (NIV) - 'Blessed are those who mourn, for they will be comforted.'
Tuesday.action: Identify one area of grief or sadness you've tried to ignore. Bring it honestly to God in a quiet moment.
Tuesday.prayer: Jesus, I bring my mourning to you. Thank you that I don't have to hide my feelings. Please be my comfort today.

Wednesday.scripture: Matthew 5:6 (NIV) - 'Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they will be filled.'
Wednesday.action: Replace one habit of consuming media or gossip with 10 minutes of reading Scripture or a devotional.
Wednesday.prayer: God, create in me a hunger for what is right, pure, and true. Fill me with your righteousness today.

Thursday.scripture: Matthew 5:5 (NIV) - 'Blessed are the meek, for they will inherit the earth.'
Thursday.action: Practice meekness by intentionally listening more than you speak in one conversation today.
Thursday.prayer: Father, help me to be meek—humble and strong under your control. Teach me to serve others with a quiet heart.

Friday.scripture: Psalm 16:11 (NIV) - 'You make known to me the path of life; you will fill me with joy in your presence, with eternal pleasures at your right hand.'
Friday.action: Spend 5 minutes in worship, thanking God for who He is, not just for what He has given you.
Friday.prayer: Jesus, my joy is in you. Remind me that my happiness is secure because it is hidden in you, my Savior.

Saturday.scripture: Genesis 35:18 (NIV) - 'As she breathed her last... she named her son Ben-Oni. But his father named him Benjamin.'
Saturday.action: Reflect on a past hurt. Write down one way God has brought strength or growth from that season.
Saturday.prayer: God, you have the power to rename my sorrows. Help me to see my story through your eyes of redemption and strength.
